user_request: Draft a suit for damages for defamation against a Defendant who published false and malicious allegations on Facebook damaging the Plaintiff's business reputation. Reproduce the defamatory statements in the body of the plaint. Plead malice, publication to third parties, loss of goodwill, and mental agony. Claim Rs.10,00,000/- as damages. Include prayer for permanent injunction restraining further publication on Facebook or any other social media platform. Draft suitable for filing before the District Court. Plead limitation correctly for defamation suits. Include cause of action paragraph with accrual date, continuing nature of cause of action, valuation, court fee, and proper verification clause.

facts.summary: Plaintiff seeks damages for defamation against a Defendant who published false and malicious allegations on Facebook, damaging the Plaintiff's business reputation. The Plaintiff claims loss of goodwill and mental agony and seeks a permanent injunction against further publication.

missing_fields: Date of publication (cause of action accrual)

Defendant's publication was accessible to third parties

arrive at a compromise whereby the first defendant is to pay Rs 6,000 and the second defendant Rs 5,000 to 
the plaintiff. The court may pass a decree in terms of the compromise, although it has jurisdiction limited to suits 
of which the value of the subject matter does not exceed Rs 5,000.14

draft_artifacts.title: Suit for Damages for Defamation and Permanent Injunction
draft_artifacts.text:
In the Court of the District Judge, {{COURT_PLACE}}

Suit No. {{SUIT_NUMBER}} of {{YEAR}}

{{PLAINTIFF_NAME}}
    ... Plaintiff

Versus

{{DEFENDANT_NAME}}
    ... Defendant

Suit for Damages for Defamation and Permanent Injunction

The Plaintiff above named submits as follows:

1. That the Plaintiff is a {{PLAINTIFF_OCCUPATION}} aged about {{PLAINTIFF_AGE}} years, residing at {{PLAINTIFF_ADDRESS}}.

2. That the Defendant is a {{DEFENDANT_OCCUPATION}} aged about {{DEFENDANT_AGE}} years, residing at {{DEFENDANT_ADDRESS}}.

3. That this Hon'ble Court has jurisdiction to entertain and try this suit as the cause of action arose within the jurisdiction of this Court and the Defendant resides/carries on business within the jurisdiction of this Court.

4. That the Plaintiff is engaged in business and enjoys good reputation and goodwill in the trade and commercial circles.

5. That in compliance with Order 6 Rule 4 of the Code of Civil Procedure, 1908, the Plaintiff gives below the particulars of the defamatory statements published by the Defendant. On or about {{DATE_OF_PUBLICATION}}, the Defendant with malicious intent and without lawful justification published the following false and defamatory statements concerning the Plaintiff on the social media platform Facebook:

"{{DEFAMATORY_STATEMENTS}}"

6. That the said publication was made to third parties including friends, followers, business associates, and members of the public who have access to the Defendant's Facebook profile/page, thereby causing grave injury to the Plaintiff's reputation and goodwill.

7. That the Defendant published the aforesaid statements with malice, ill-will, and with knowledge of their falsity or with reckless disregard for the truth, intending to harm the Plaintiff's business reputation.

8. That by reason of the said false and malicious publication, the Plaintiff has suffered loss of goodwill, damage to business reputation, and has undergone severe mental agony and humiliation.

9. That the Plaintiff has suffered pecuniary loss and damages to the extent of Rs. 10,00,000/- (Rupees Ten Lakhs only).

10. That the cause of action first arose on {{DATE_OF_PUBLICATION}}, when the Defendant published the defamatory statements on Facebook, thereby violating the Plaintiff's right to reputation. The right to sue accrued to the Plaintiff on the said date under Article {{LIMITATION_ARTICLE}} of the Schedule to the Limitation Act, 1963. The cause of action is of a continuing nature inasmuch as the defamatory publication continues to remain accessible on the said platform causing continuing injury to the Plaintiff.

11. That the Plaintiff has served a legal notice dated {{NOTICE_DATE}} upon the Defendant calling upon the Defendant to remove the defamatory content and pay damages, but the Defendant has failed to comply with the same.

12. That the Plaintiff apprehends that the Defendant may continue to publish similar defamatory content on Facebook and/or other social media platforms, causing further irreparable harm to the Plaintiff's reputation.

13. That the Plaintiff values the suit for the purpose of jurisdiction and court fee at Rs. 10,00,000/- (Rupees Ten Lakhs only). The requisite court fee of {{COURT_FEE_AMOUNT}} has been paid on the plaint as per the Court Fees Act, 1870.

Prayer

In view of the foregoing, the Plaintiff most respectfully prays that this Hon'ble Court may be pleased to:

(a) Pass a decree for damages of Rs. 10,00,000/- (Rupees Ten Lakhs only) in favour of the Plaintiff and against the Defendant;

(b) Grant a permanent injunction restraining the Defendant, his agents, servants, or anyone claiming through him from publishing, circulating, or causing to be published any defamatory statements concerning the Plaintiff on Facebook or any other social media platform;

(c) Pass any other order or direction as this Hon'ble Court may deem fit and proper in the facts and circumstances of the case; and

(d) Award costs of the suit to the Plaintiff.

Verification

I, {{PLAINTIFF_NAME}}, the Plaintiff above named, do hereby verify that the contents of paragraphs 1 to 13 of the foregoing plaint are true to my knowledge, and the contents of paragraph 5 (reproducing the defamatory statements) are based on the record of the Facebook post.

Verified at {{COURT_PLACE}} on this {{DATE_OF_VERIFICATION}} day of {{MONTH}}, {{YEAR}}.

List of Documents

1. Screenshot/printout of the defamatory Facebook post - Annexure P-1
2. Legal notice dated {{NOTICE_DATE}} - Annexure P-2
3. Proof of service of legal notice - Annexure P-3
4. Reply to legal notice (if any) - Annexure P-4

That this Hon'ble Court has the territorial and pecuniary jurisdiction to entertain and try the present suit because (i) the Defendant resides and/or works for gain within the jurisdiction of this Hon'ble Court at {{DEFENDANT_ADDRESS_WITHIN_JURISDICTION}}, and (ii) the defamatory content was published on Facebook and was accessible to and read by third parties within the jurisdiction of this Hon'ble Court at {{COURT_PLACE}}, and (iii) the Plaintiff carries on business and enjoys goodwill and reputation within the jurisdiction of this Hon'ble Court at {{PLAINTIFF_BUSINESS_PLACE_WITHIN_JURISDICTION}}, where the Plaintiff’s reputation and business interests have been harmed.

4. That the Plaintiff is engaged in business and enjoys good reputation and goodwill in the trade and commercial circles.

5. That in compliance with Order VI Rule 4 of the Code of Civil Procedure, 1908, the Plaintiff provides below the particulars of the defamatory publication. On or about {{DATE_OF_PUBLICATION}} at about {{TIME_OF_PUBLICATION}} (approx.), the Defendant, using his Facebook account/page namely {{DEFENDANT_FACEBOOK_ACCOUNT_NAME}} (Profile/Page URL: {{DEFENDANT_FACEBOOK_PROFILE_URL}}), published and/or caused to be published the following false and defamatory statements concerning the Plaintiff, by way of a post available at {{FACEBOOK_POST_URL}} ("Impugned Post"):

    5.1 "{{DEFAMATORY_STATEMENT_1}}"

    5.2 "{{DEFAMATORY_STATEMENT_2}}"

    5.3 "{{DEFAMATORY_STATEMENT_3}}"

(Collectively, the "Defamatory Statements"). A true copy/screenshot/printout of the Impugned Post is filed herewith as Annexure P-1.

6. That the said publication was made to and seen by third parties including friends, followers, business associates, customers, prospective customers, and members of the public who have access to the Defendant's Facebook profile/page, thereby causing grave injury to the Plaintiff's reputation and goodwill.

7. That the Defendant published the aforesaid statements with malice, ill-will, and without any lawful justification, and with knowledge of their falsity or with reckless disregard for the truth, intending to harm the Plaintiff's business reputation.

8. That by reason of the said false and malicious publication, the Plaintiff has suffered loss of goodwill, damage to business reputation, and has undergone severe mental agony and humiliation.

9. That the Plaintiff has suffered pecuniary loss and damages which the Plaintiff reasonably quantifies at Rs. 10,00,000/- (Rupees Ten Lakhs only).

10. That the cause of action first arose on {{DATE_OF_PUBLICATION}}, when the Defendant published the Defamatory Statements on Facebook, thereby violating the Plaintiff's right to reputation. The right to sue accrued to the Plaintiff on the said date. The present suit is within limitation as it is being filed within the prescribed limitation period applicable to civil claims for defamation, i.e., within one year from the date of such publication/right to sue having accrued. The Plaintiff further states that so long as the Impugned Post remains accessible on Facebook, the Plaintiff continues to suffer ongoing and recurring injury to reputation and goodwill.

11. That the Plaintiff, through counsel, served a legal notice dated {{NOTICE_DATE}} upon the Defendant calling upon the Defendant to immediately remove/disable access to the defamatory content and to pay damages. The said legal notice is annexed as Annexure P-2 and proof of service is annexed as Annexure P-3. The Defendant has failed to comply. The reply, if any, is annexed as Annexure P-4.

12. That the Plaintiff apprehends that the Defendant may continue to publish similar or further defamatory content on Facebook and/or other social media platforms, causing further harm to the Plaintiff's reputation.

13. That the Plaintiff values the suit for the purpose of jurisdiction and court fee as follows:

    13.1 For the relief of damages: Rs. 10,00,000/- (Rupees Ten Lakhs only);

    13.2 For the relief of permanent injunction: Rs. {{INJUNCTION_RELIEF_VALUE}} (as valued for the purposes of court fee and jurisdiction as per applicable law).

The court fee payable is as per {{APPLICABLE_COURT_FEES_STATUTE}} / applicable court-fee law in force and the rules of this Hon'ble Court. Court fee of {{COURT_FEE_AMOUNT}} is being affixed/paid on the plaint, subject to verification by the Registry.

Prayer

In view of the foregoing, the Plaintiff most respectfully prays that this Hon'ble Court may be pleased to:

(a) Pass a decree for damages of Rs. 10,00,000/- (Rupees Ten Lakhs only) in favour of the Plaintiff and against the Defendant;

(b) Grant a permanent injunction restraining the Defendant, his agents, servants, or anyone claiming through him from publishing, circulating, uploading, sharing, or causing to be published any defamatory statements concerning the Plaintiff on Facebook or any other social media platform;

(c) Pass any other order or direction as this Hon'ble Court may deem fit and proper in the facts and circumstances of the case; and

(d) Award costs of the suit to the Plaintiff.

Verification

I, {{PLAINTIFF_NAME}}, the Plaintiff above named, do hereby verify that the contents of paragraphs 1 to 13 of the foregoing plaint are true to my knowledge, information and belief, as the case may be; and that the Defamatory Statements reproduced in paragraph 5 are true reproductions from the Facebook post(s) evidenced by Annexure P-1.

Verified at {{COURT_PLACE}} on this {{DATE_OF_VERIFICATION}} day of {{MONTH}}, {{YEAR}}.

List of Documents

1. Screenshot/printout of the defamatory Facebook post (Impugned Post) along with URL/permalink details, if available — Annexure P-1
2. Legal notice dated {{NOTICE_DATE}} — Annexure P-2
3. Proof of service of legal notice — Annexure P-3
4. Reply to legal notice (if any) — Annexure P-4

blocking_issues.issue: Statutory reference cited without RAG support — may be sourced from training memory which can produce incorrect provision numbers for this cause of action type
blocking_issues.fix: Replace the unsupported citation with a placeholder (e.g., {{PROVISION_NAME}}) and record the reason; verify the correct provision before filing
blocking_issues.location: Paragraph 13 ("as per the Court Fees Act, 1870")

blocking_issues.issue: Limitation pleading not stated with a verified limitation period; placeholder limitation article used and limitation compliance is not clearly pleaded
blocking_issues.fix: Remove the placeholder article reference and plead limitation in substance: suit is filed within one year from the date of publication/knowledge; keep accrual date tied to the publication date; plead continuing accessibility only as continuing injury without incorrectly extending limitation

blocking_issues.issue: Document reference label mismatch: label in body does not match List of Documents
blocking_issues.fix: Ensure every document label in the body matches exactly the corresponding list entry; use a single consistent label scheme throughout the draft
blocking_issues.location: Body (Paras 5, 11, Verification) and List of Documents (Annexure P-1 to P-4)

blocking_issues.issue: Jurisdiction averment is conclusory and may be insufficient for a District Court plaint; it does not specify the jurisdictional facts (place of publication/where it was read, where Plaintiff carries on business, where Defendant resides/works for gain)
blocking_issues.fix: Amend jurisdiction paragraph to plead specific jurisdictional facts using placeholders tied to the factual matrix (e.g., Defendant resides/works for gain at {{DEFENDANT_ADDRESS}} within jurisdiction; post was published/viewed within {{COURT_PLACE}}; Plaintiff’s business and reputation were harmed within jurisdiction)

blocking_issues.issue: Defamatory statements are not reproduced verbatim in the plaint as required by the user request; the draft currently contains only a placeholder
blocking_issues.fix: Insert the exact defamatory words/lines verbatim (preferably as separate sub-paragraphs) along with identifiers (post date/time, URL/permalink, account/page name) using placeholders where facts are not provided; ensure the statements are capable of being read as defamatory on their face

non_blocking_issues.issue: Relief for injunction is pleaded, but the plaint does not expressly plead why damages are an inadequate remedy / why injunction is necessary beyond apprehension; adding a short irreparable harm / balance of convenience pleading would strengthen the plaint
non_blocking_issues.fix: Optionally add 1–2 sentences supporting injunction (continuing accessibility, speed of virality, inability to fully quantify reputational harm) without changing the cause of action

non_blocking_issues.issue: The plaint does not capture technical identifiers of the alleged publication (Facebook profile/page name, handle, post ID/permalink, screenshot metadata). This may affect proof later though not strictly mandatory at filing stage
non_blocking_issues.fix: Optionally add placeholders for URL/permalink/post ID and refer to them in Annexure P-1 description
non_blocking_issues.location: Paragraph 5 and List of Documents item 1
